content: The emergent clustering of high-value information in 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems follows the same self-organizing principle as the social division of labor within a collective of generalist agents: only the most uncertain, high-impact unlabeled data samples are prioritized for retention and labeling, just as only the highest-utility, unaddressed tasks in a collective agent system are allocated to specialized agent roles to maximize overall system efficiency. This creates a nested 'memory as a collective of data' framework where an individual agent's memory behaves like a microcosm of a multi-agent collective.
